quiet.description: suppress progress bar and log output

cacert.description: Path to PEM-encoded CA certificate file

ttl.description: cache TTL when using redis as cache backend

tls.description: enable redis TLS with public certificates, if using redis as cache backend

ca.description: redis ca file location, if using redis as cache backend

cert.description: redis certificate file location, if using redis as cache backend

key.description: redis key file location, if using redis as cache backend

scan-cache.description: remove scan cache (container and VM image analysis results)

token-header.description: specify a header name for token in client/server mode

download-only.description: download/update vulnerability database but don't run a scan

download-java-only.description: download/update Java index database but don't run a scan

repository.description: OCI repository(ies) to retrieve trivy-db in order of priority

java-repository.description: OCI repository(ies) to retrieve trivy-java-db in order of priority

input.description: input file path instead of image name

image-config-scanners.description: comma-separated list of what security issues to detect on container image configurations

removed-pkgs.description: detect vulnerabilities of removed packages (only for Alpine)

platform.description: set platform in the form os/arch if image is multi-platform capable

host.description: unix domain socket path to use for docker scanning
unix podman socket path to use for podman scanning

source.description: image source(s) to use, in priority order

max-size.description: [EXPERIMENTAL] maximum image size to process, specified in a human-readable format (e.g., '44kB', '17MB'); an error will be returned if the image exceeds this size

kubeconfig.description: specify the kubeconfig file path to use

k8s-version.description: specify k8s version to validate outdated api by it (example: 1.21.0)

disableNodeCollector.description: When the flag is activated, the node-collector job will not be executed, thus skipping misconfiguration findings on the node.

namespace.description: specify the namespace in which the node-collector job should be deployed

imageref.description: indicate the image reference for the node-collector scan job

owned.description: exclude resources that have an owner reference

nodes.description: indicate the node labels that the node-collector job should exclude from scanning (example: kubernetes.io/arch:arm64,team:dev)

excludeKinds.description: indicate the kinds exclude from scanning (example: node)

includeKinds.description: indicate the kinds included in scanning (example: node)

excludeNamespaces.description: indicate the namespaces excluded from scanning (example: kube-system)

includeNamespaces.description: indicate the namespaces included in scanning (example: kube-system)

qps.description: specify the maximum QPS to the master from this client

skipImages.description: skip the downloading and scanning of images (vulnerabilities and secrets) in the cluster resources

burst.description: specify the maximum burst for throttle

full.description: eagerly look for licenses in source code headers and license files

ignored.description: specify a list of license to ignore

checks-bundle-repository.description: OCI registry URL to retrieve checks bundle from

set.description: specify Helm values on the command line (can specify multiple or separate values with commas: key1=val1,key2=val2)

values.description: specify paths to override the Helm values.yaml files

set-file.description: specify Helm values from respective files specified via the command line (can specify multiple or separate values with commas: key1=path1,key2=path2)

set-string.description: specify Helm string values on the command line (can specify multiple or separate values with commas: key1=val1,key2=val2)

api-versions.description: Available API versions used for Capabilities.APIVersions. This flag is the same as the api-versions flag of the helm template command. (can specify multiple or separate values with commas: policy/v1/PodDisruptionBudget,apps/v1/Deployment)

kube-version.description: Kubernetes version used for Capabilities.KubeVersion. This flag is the same as the kube-version flag of the helm template command.

vars.description: specify paths to override the Terraform tfvars files

exclude-downloaded-modules.description: exclude misconfigurations for downloaded terraform modules

params.description: specify paths to override the CloudFormation parameters files

scanners.description: comma-separated list of misconfig scanners to use for misconfiguration scanning

config-file-schemas.description: specify paths to JSON configuration file schemas to determine that a file matches some configuration and pass the schema to Rego checks for type checking

render-cause.description: specify configuration types for which the rendered causes will be shown in the table report

raw-config-scanners.description: specify the types of scanners that will also scan raw configurations. For example, scanners will scan a non-adapted configuration into a shared state

playbooks.description: specify playbook file path(s) to scan

inventories.description: specify inventory host path or comma separated host list

extra-vars.description: set additional variables as key=value or @file (YAML/JSON)

dir.description: specify directory to the wasm modules that will be loaded

include-dev-deps.description: include development dependencies in the report (supported: npm, yarn, gradle)

password.description: password. Comma-separated passwords allowed. TRIVY_PASSWORD should be used for security reasons.

password-stdin.description: password from stdin. Comma-separated passwords are not supported.

mirrors.description: map of hosts and registries for them.

trace.description: enable more verbose trace output for custom queries

check.description: specify the paths to the Rego check files or to the directories containing them, applying config files

data.description: specify paths from which data for the Rego checks will be recursively loaded

error-limit.description: maximum number of compile errors allowed during Rego policy evaluation

report.description: specify a report format for the output

template.description: output template (file path must have .tpl extension)

dependency-tree.description: [EXPERIMENTAL] show dependency origin tree of vulnerable packages

list-all-pkgs.description: output all packages in the JSON report regardless of vulnerability

ignore-policy.description: specify the Rego file path to evaluate each vulnerability

exit-code.description: specify exit code when any security issues are found

exit-on-eol.description: exit with the specified code when the OS reaches end of service/life

severity.description: severities of security issues to be displayed

skip-dirs.description: specify the directories or glob patterns to skip

skip-files.description: specify the files or glob patterns to skip

offline.description: do not issue API requests to identify dependencies

scanners.description: comma-separated list of what security issues to detect

parallel.description: number of goroutines enabled for parallel scanning, set 0 to auto-detect parallelism

sbom-sources.description: [EXPERIMENTAL] try to retrieve SBOM from the specified sources

detection-priority.description:
specify the detection priority:
  - "precise": Prioritizes precise by minimizing false positives.
  - "comprehensive": Aims to detect more security findings at the cost of potential false positives.

skip-version-check.description: suppress notices about version updates and Trivy announcements

disable-telemetry.description: disable sending anonymous usage data to Aqua

table-mode.description: [EXPERIMENTAL] tables that will be displayed in 'table' format

branch.description: pass the branch name to be scanned

commit.description: pass the commit hash to be scanned

tag.description: pass the tag name to be scanned

config.description: specify a path to config file for secret scanning

ignore-status.description: comma-separated list of vulnerability status to ignore

severity-source.description: order of data sources for selecting vulnerability severity level

title: Trivy Configuration
description: Configuration file for Trivy security scanner (trivy.yaml)
